description: Command palette built on cmdk. cmdk renders an accessible combobox: CommandInput is a text input with aria-autocomplete="list" and aria-controls pointing at CommandList (role="listbox"); each CommandItem is role="option". CommandDialog wraps the whole thing inside a modal Dialog so the typical invocation is a keyboard-shortcut-opened palette.

keyboard.action: When inside CommandDialog, focus stays trapped in the dialog. Tab cycles through the input and any dialog chrome.
Moves the highlighted option down the list (wraps at the end).
Moves the highlighted option up the list (wraps at the start).
Closes the dialog (when used as CommandDialog); returns focus to the trigger.

focus.notes: Focus stays in the CommandInput throughout navigation; cmdk uses aria-activedescendant to move the virtual cursor through the list without stealing DOM focus from the input. When wrapped in CommandDialog, Radix Dialog traps focus inside the popup.

announcements: The input announces as a combobox with its current typed value.
Each CommandItem announces as an option; the active descendant is announced as the user arrows through the list.
CommandEmpty content is announced when the list is empty after filtering.
screenReader.notes: cmdk uses aria-activedescendant, which most modern screen readers handle well but some older AT combos read less fluidly. Keep item labels self-contained (don't rely on icons alone to convey meaning).

contrast.text: Item text uses foreground over bg-popover; muted-foreground for group headings and the search icon. All meet WCAG AA
contrast.ui: Selected item uses bg-accent / text-accent-foreground which meets AA

motion.reducedMotion: When wrapped in CommandDialog, the dialog's enter / exit animations apply (see Dialog). cmdk itself does not animate.

consumerResponsibilities: Provide a clear placeholder on CommandInput so users understand what to type.
Use CommandGroup headings to organize long lists; screen reader users benefit from the structure.
Ensure the palette is reachable via an obvious keyboard shortcut (e.g. Cmd / Ctrl + K) and document it.
